articles.title: Could the next Chinese threat walk into your kitchen on two battery-powered legs?
articles.summary: Humanoid robots could be in every American home within a decade. Experts warn the U.S. must win the robotics race against China for national security.

articles.title: Here's why universal basic income would be a disaster for America’s future
articles.summary: As AI transforms the economy, some tech leaders push universal basic income. This piece argues Americans need opportunity, not government checks.

articles.title: SEN KEVIN CRAMER: China builds for war while America waits on permits
articles.summary: Permitting reform is essential to rebuild America's defense industrial base and deter conflict with China, which outproduces the U.S. in key areas.

articles.title: Top takeaways from the primary elections in Maine and South Carolina: 'Movement about us'
articles.summary: Graham Platner cruises to Maine Democratic Senate nomination despite mounting controversies, setting up a key midterm battle against Sen. Susan Collins.

articles.title: Judge blocks Alabama's nitrogen gas execution method, rules it is unconstitutionally cruel
articles.summary: A federal judge permanently blocked Alabama from using nitrogen gas for executions, ruling the method violates the ban on cruel and unusual punishment.

articles.title: Trump-endorsed candidate will face top GOP target in Nevada House district
articles.summary: Michael Boris scores upset victory in Nevada's 1st Congressional District Republican primary, defeating Trump-backed state Sen. Carrie Buck. OR Trump-endorsed state Sen. Carrie Buck wins Republican primary to take on Rep. Dina Titus, D-Nev., in Nevada's first congressional district.

articles.title: Gaming-world veteran who ripped ‘woke’ culture scores Trump-backed battleground primary win
articles.summary: Trump-endorsed Halo composer Marty O'Donnell advances in GOP primary to challenge Democratic incumbent Susie Lee in Nevada's 3rd congressional district.

articles.title: Top GOP target Dina Titus fends off House primary challengers
articles.summary: Rep. Dina Titus wins the Democratic primary in Nevada's 1st Congressional District, defeating three challengers to secure her eighth term nomination.

articles.title: Nevada’s top cop wins Dem primary, takes on Gov Lombardo
articles.summary: Nevada's Democratic primary for governor sets up a showdown with GOP Gov. Joe Lombardo in one of the nation's most competitive gubernatorial races.

articles.title: Dem rep who went on expletive-laden anti-Trump rant wins primary
articles.summary: Rep. Susie Lee faced challengers in Nevada's 3rd Congressional District Democratic primary, one of the West's most competitive swing districts.

articles.title: Joe Lombardo cruises past six GOP challengers to secure Nevada Republican gubernatorial nomination
articles.summary: Nevada Gov. Joe Lombardo wins the Republican primary for a second term, defeating six GOP challengers to advance to the general election in November.

articles.title: Jordan Staal's two-goal night lifts Hurricanes past Golden Knights, evening Stanley Cup Final series
articles.summary: Jordan Staal scored twice as the Carolina Hurricanes beat the Golden Knights 5-3 in Game 4 to even the Stanley Cup Final series at 2-2 heading home.

articles.title: South Carolina GOP race to replace Nancy Mace heads to runoff
articles.summary: Jenny Honeycut and Mark Smith advance to a June 23 GOP runoff in South Carolina's 1st Congressional District to replace outgoing Rep. Nancy Mace.

articles.title: Collins secures GOP nod in Maine Senate battle that could decide GOP majority
articles.summary: Sen. Susan Collins wins Maine GOP primary unopposed as she seeks a sixth term, facing Democrat Graham Platner in a crucial Senate majority battle.

articles.title: Kentucky football mourns Nic Smith after defensive lineman found dead on campus
articles.summary: Kentucky defensive lineman Nic Smith has died at age 20 after being found in a residence hall. Initial investigation indicates no foul play involved.

articles.title: Trump administration to offer 'premium' expedited visa interviews for $750
articles.summary: The State Department will unveil a $750 premium service allowing foreigners seeking U.S. business or tourist visas to schedule interviews within 10 days of payment.

articles.title: Judge dismisses lawsuit by 31 former NC State athletes alleging sexual abuse, misconduct by ex-head trainer
articles.summary: A North Carolina judge dismissed a lawsuit by 31 ex-N.C. State athletes alleging sexual abuse by the program's former sports medicine director.

articles.title: Netflix film chief says they won't work with directors who want to release movies in theaters

articles.title: Dem who vows to impeach RFK Jr clinches nomination in key Senate race to
articles.summary: Dr. Annie Andrews, a pediatrician, wins South Carolina's Democratic primary to challenge Sen. Lindsey Graham in his bid for a fifth Senate term.

articles.title: Bernie Sanders dodges questions about abuse allegations facing Maine Senate candidate Platner
articles.summary: Sen. Bernie Sanders dodged questions about abuse allegations facing Maine Democratic Senate candidate Graham Platner, refusing to respond at the Capitol.

articles.title: Trump-backed Hilton advances to California governor general election
articles.summary: Democrat Javier Becerra and Trump-backed Republican Steve Hilton will face off in November for California governor, succeeding Gavin Newsom.

articles.title: Fetterman refuses to defend Platner, calls him 'baggage' as scandals mount
articles.summary: Sen. John Fetterman breaks with Democrats, refuses to defend Maine Senate candidate Graham Platner amid mounting controversies over online conduct.

articles.title: JD Vance installs custom chicken coop at Naval Observatory and will personally care for birds
articles.summary: A custom chicken coop built by Carolina Coops has been installed at the Naval Observatory, where Vice President JD Vance will care for the chickens.

articles.title: Platner holdout floats emergency lifeline for panicking Dems if scandal-plagued candidate wins
articles.summary: Rep. Josh Gottheimer calls for Maine Senate candidate Graham Platner to exit the race, saying he should get off the ballot and let a Democrat step in.

articles.title: Trump locks in ICE funding through end of presidency after House passes $70B package
articles.summary: Republicans' $70 billion Secure America Act cleared the House, funding ICE and Border Patrol through 2029 despite unanimous Democratic opposition.

articles.title: Obama pays tribute to chef who tragically drowned at vacation compound with new honor
articles.summary: Obama Presidential Center launches Tafari's Kitchen, a comfort-food restaurant honoring late personal chef Tafari Campbell who drowned in 2023.

articles.title: Bystanders hailed as 'heroic' after intervening in brutal knife attack by Sudanese migrant in UK
articles.summary: A Sudanese man was arrested on suspicion of attempted murder after a knife attack in Belfast, Northern Ireland left a man in his 40s with serious injuries.

articles.title: Iran accelerates execution campaign against anti-regime activists amid internet censorship
articles.summary: Iran accelerates executions of dissidents and protesters amid internet censorship, with actual numbers almost certainly higher than documented figures.

articles.title: Finland’s foreign minister says Ukraine ‘is now holding the cards’ as Russia signals talks
articles.summary: Finnish Foreign Minister Elina Valtonen says Ukraine is holding the cards today as Russia renews talk of negotiations amid Kyiv's military gains.

articles.title: 'You're destroying your countries': Is Europe finally heeding Trump's warning on illegal immigration?
articles.summary: Trump and Vance warned EU about illegal immigration as new stricter border rules emerge as Europe's politicians seem to be reacting to the needs of its citizens.

articles.title: US adversaries China, North Korea strengthening ties as Xi, Kim set to begin talks
articles.summary: Chinese President Xi Jinping arrived in Pyongyang for a rare state visit, set to meet with North Korea's Kim Jong Un as both nations seek to restore their traditional alliance.

articles.title: Hezbollah's secret 'kill, wound and maim' bomb network exposed as Israel strikes Beirut
articles.summary: Israel launched retaliatory airstrikes on Hezbollah command centers in Beirut after rockets were fired into northern Israel, marking a major escalation.

articles.title: Iran admits extraordinary new detail in Khamenei strike, Trump offered 'way out': expert
articles.summary: Iran's foreign minister reveals new details about the strike that killed Supreme Leader Khamenei, confirming U.S.-Israeli precision targeting.

articles.title: Suspected Hamas terrorist arrested in Greece for allegedly plotting attack on Israeli cruise ship
articles.summary: A suspected Hamas terrorist reportedly granted asylum in Greece was arrested in Crete for allegedly plotting an attack on an Israeli cruise ship.

articles.title: Americans travel to Pakistan to free Christians trapped in modern-day slavery: 'God's hand was in it'
articles.summary: Aaron Hutchings says he was shocked to see children making bricks under the hot sun in Pakistan to pay off family debts that had accumulated over generations.

articles.title: NATO's eastern flank races to rearm as Trump pressure exposes Western Europe's defense gap
articles.summary: Eastern European NATO allies closest to Russia are spending the most on defense, while Western Europe faces pressure to meet the alliance's 5% GDP goal.

articles.title: Pro-US conservative faces leftist in Peru's high-stakes presidential runoff
articles.summary: Keiko Fujimori and Roberto Sánchez face off in Peru's pivotal presidential runoff, a race that could determine the country's alignment with Washington or a leftward shift.

articles.title: Pope Leo XIV jokes young Spaniards would pick Bad Bunny over him during Madrid visit this weekend
articles.summary: Pope Leo XIV joked that young Spaniards might choose Bad Bunny over him during his Madrid visit, but an estimated 500,000 gathered for a vigil.

articles.title: Spearfisherman killed by suspected 15-foot shark after third fatal attack in less than a month
articles.summary: A 35-year-old spearfisherman was killed by a suspected 15-foot shark off Western Australia, marking the third fatal attack there in under a month.

articles.title: World War II veteran reveals 1-word feeling before D-Day 82 years later
articles.summary: WWII veteran Arthur Rose read a letter written days after D-Day at an 82nd anniversary ceremony in Normandy, France, recounting the historic invasion.

articles.title: Jeff Bartos says UN reform is no longer an 'oxymoron' after $570M in cuts
articles.summary: Jeff Bartos details $570 million in U.N. budget cuts and 2,900 eliminated positions as the Trump administration pushes historic reform efforts.

articles.title: Raúl Castro makes first public appearance since Trump administration charged him with murder
articles.summary: Raúl Castro made his first public appearance since Trump charged him with murder over the 1996 shoot-down of Brothers to the Rescue planes in Cuba.

articles.title: US, Shield of the Americas condemn 'ongoing efforts' to overthrow Bolivia's elected president amid unrest
articles.summary: The U.S. and the Shield of the Americas countries condemned ongoing efforts to overthrow Bolivia's elected President Rodrigo Paz amid weeks of mass protests.

articles.title: Zelenskyy issues open letter to Putin proposing meeting as US 'fully focused' on Iran
articles.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy proposes meeting with Russian President Vladimir Putin to negotiate end of years-long Russia-Ukraine war.

articles.title: Pete Hegseth warns narco-terrorists as US backs Bolivia's government amid coup warnings
articles.summary: War Secretary Pete Hegseth says the U.S. remains committed to defending Bolivia's government amid coup warnings and mass unrest over fuel prices.

articles.title: As Hezbollah rejects truce, families on Israel's northern border describe life under fire
articles.summary: Residents of Israel's northern border describe life under what they call "the ceasefire war," with sirens and explosions despite agreements.

articles.title: Canada's Carney pledges action on antisemitism amid backlash over new anti-hate council members
articles.summary: Prime Minister Mark Carney's new council to combat antisemitism draws backlash over members critics say are hostile to Israel and Jewish concerns.

articles.title: Sherpa missing for a week on Everest found crawling toward base camp after his family begins funeral rites
articles.summary: Rescuers call it a miracle after a Sherpa guide survived nearly a week alone on Mount Everest without food, water or supplemental oxygen in the death zone.

articles.title: Albino buffalo nicknamed 'Donald Trump' becomes sensation at Bangladesh's national zoo for its blond hair
articles.summary: A rare albino buffalo nicknamed "Donald Trump" has become a sensation at Bangladesh's national zoo thanks to its blond hair resembling the president.

articles.title: Trump expands Cuba sanctions beyond US companies in major crackdown on foreign enablers
articles.summary: Trump administration rolls out unprecedented secondary sanctions on Cuba targeting foreign firms doing business with military-linked conglomerate GAESA.

articles.title: Bodycam footage fuels backlash after police handcuff dying stabbing victim
articles.summary: Bodycam footage shows English police handcuffing 18-year-old Henry Nowak after he said he was stabbed and could not breathe moments before dying.

articles.title: Judge blocks Alabama's nitrogen gas execution method, rules it is unconstitutionally cruel
articles.summary: A federal judge permanently blocked Alabama from using nitrogen gas for executions, ruling the method violates the ban on cruel and unusual punishment.

articles.title: LISTEN: Brian Hooker's own words appear to place him in the water the night missing wife vanished in Bahamas
articles.summary: A recorded phone call from Brian Hooker days after wife Lynette vanished in the Bahamas suggests he was in the water with her, contradicting his account.

articles.title: DHS directs ICE to deport illegal immigrants who vote in American elections under new directive
articles.summary: DHS directs ICE to deport undocumented immigrants who vote in U.S. elections, enforcing stricter penalties for illegal ballot casting by noncitizens.

articles.title: Nancy Guthrie sheriff caught with undeclared gun at airport faced no charges, bodycam video shows
articles.summary: Bodycam video shows Pima County Sheriff Chris Nanos receiving a Miranda warning after bringing a loaded gun to a TSA checkpoint at Tucson's airport.

articles.title: Karmelo Anthony found guilty of murder in Texas track meet stabbing
articles.summary: A Collin County jury convicted Karmelo Anthony on a murder charge for the fatal stabbing of 17-year-old Austin Metcalf at a Frisco track meet.

articles.title: Navy veteran among trio who allegedly wanted to behead soldier and fund ISIS drone attacks on Americans
articles.summary: Three men, including a U.S. Navy veteran, are charged with allegedly conspiring to provide material support to ISIS and allegedly plotting violence against troops.

articles.title: WATCH: Surveillance video captures Penn State student's final moments before fatal shooting
articles.summary: Surveillance video captures Penn State student Billy Schmidt's final moments before he was fatally shot during an armed robbery attempt in Philadelphia.

articles.title: Charlotte train stabbing suspect dodges potential death penalty for now after he's ruled incompetent
articles.summary: Decarlos Brown Jr., accused of fatally stabbing Ukrainian woman Iryna Zarutska on a Charlotte light-rail train, is deemed incompetent to stand trial.

articles.title: Utah prosecutors ask judge to shut down delay tactics in Charlie Kirk assassination case
articles.summary: Utah prosecutors urge judge to reject defense delay in Charlie Kirk assassination case, arguing the suspect's lawyers failed to meet stay criteria.

articles.title: Texas’ largest school district sees test scores soar after state takeover despite racism claims
articles.summary: Houston ISD sees dramatic improvements in student grades and test scores across more than 270 campuses in the years since Texas state takeover began in 2023.

articles.title: Maine Democrats decide fate of Senate candidate dogged by explosive allegations and more top headlines
articles.summary: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.

articles.title: Karmelo Anthony jury begins deliberating, to consider manslaughter charge in track meet murder trial
articles.summary: Karmelo Anthony's defense rests without his testimony in the murder trial for the stabbing death of Austin Metcalf at a Texas high school track meet.

articles.title: Navy sailor admits killing fellow service member as mother questions missed warning signs
articles.summary: Navy sailor Jeremiah Copeland pleaded guilty to strangling fellow sailor Angelina Resendiz in his barracks room, facing a minimum of 40 years in prison.

articles.title: DHS approves plan to verify voter citizenship, monitor mail ballots as Trump push intensifies
articles.summary: DHS approves a plan to let states verify voter citizenship using federal immigration databases and monitor mail ballot flows for potential fraud.

articles.title: Rogue swimmer arrested after diving into 11-acre Central Park lake in failed attempt to escape NYPD
articles.summary: A 23-year-old suspect fled into Central Park and dove into Harlem Meer to evade NYPD officers attempting to arrest him on petty larceny charges.

articles.title: American Airlines flight attendant killed in tourist hot spot as investigators probe suspected boat strike
articles.summary: American Airlines flight attendant Kellie Williams, 31, was found dead on a Florida beach after an apparent boat strike while snorkeling, officials said.

articles.title: Wisconsin teen sentenced to life in brutal slaying of 5-year-old boy found in dumpster
articles.summary: Erik Mendoza, 18, was reportedly sentenced to life in prison for the 2023 brutal killing of 5-year-old Prince McCree in Milwaukee, Wisconsin.

articles.title: DOJ moves to strip citizenship from 17 people accused of hiding disturbing crimes
articles.summary: DOJ seeks to revoke citizenship of 17 individuals accused of child sexual abuse, narcotics trafficking and fraud who allegedly lied to obtain U.S. status.

articles.title: Repeat offender on probation allegedly kills father who tracked his stolen truck using GPS
articles.summary: A Houston father was killed during a carjacking after tracking his stolen truck using GPS, police said. A repeat offender on probation has been charged with murder.

articles.title: Alex Murdaugh murder case gets new judge as retrial looms following Supreme Court reversal
articles.summary: Judge Debra R. McCaslin assigned to oversee Alex Murdaugh's murder retrial after South Carolina Supreme Court overturned his convictions in May.

articles.title: New video shows Coast Guard's Bahamas hunt as team dives into forensics exam of seized Lynette Hooker dinghy
articles.summary: U.S. Coast Guard releases new video from their search for missing woman Lynette Hooker, who allegedly fell overboard in the Bahamas in April.

articles.title: Pima County sheriff issues alert for kidnapping suspect less than 10 miles from Nancy Guthrie's home
articles.summary: Pima County Sheriff's Department issues alert for kidnapping suspect Coral Michelle Smith, wanted for incident less than 10 miles from Nancy Guthrie's home.

articles.title: Four dead and 29 shot in Chicago weekend violence as leaders tout crime progress
articles.summary: Four people died and 29 were shot in numerous shootings over three days in Chicago, including victims as young as 12 years old, police report.

articles.title: Siren's Curse roller coaster strands riders vertically twice in one weekend at Six Flags Cedar Point
articles.summary: Siren's Curse at Six Flags Cedar Point was stranded twice over the weekend, leaving riders suspended vertically on North America's tallest tilt coaster.

articles.title: Manhunt underway after college student fatally shot chasing robbers who allegedly stole his phone
articles.summary: Penn State student Billy Schmidt, 22, was shot and killed in Philadelphia during an alleged armed robbery attempt while walking home from a bar.
